races.long_description: Representative Kevin Kiley, a Republican turned independent, faces an uphill battle in a newly redrawn Sacramento-area district. A host of Democrats, including Richard Pan, a physician and former state legislator, and Thien Ho, Sacramento County’s district attorney, are running against him.

In the San Francisco contest to replace Representative Nancy Pelosi, the former speaker who is retiring, State Senator Scott Wiener is the front-runner. But he faces challengers from his left, most notably Saikat Chakrabarti, a former chief of staff for Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, and Connie Chan, a member of the city’s Board of Supervisors.

Representative David Valadao, a Central Valley Republican, is running for re-election in a district that became even more vulnerable after redistricting. His two main opponents are Randy Villegas, a progressive backed by Senator Bernie Sanders, and Jasmeet Bains, a more moderate Democratic state lawmaker and physician.

Two Republican incumbents, Representatives Young Kim and Ken Calvert, were drawn into the same district by Proposition 50. They are fighting for political survival but face a number of Democrats — including Esther Kim Varet, an art dealer — who could split the vote and ensure both Republicans advance to November.

Democrats hope to gain this district, made newly competitive by Proposition 50. When Representative Darrell Issa, a Republican targeted by redistricting, elected to retire, Republicans endorsed Jim Desmond, a county supervisor. The main Democratic candidates are Ammar Campa-Najjar, a longtime local candidate, and Marni von Wilpert, a San Diego councilwoman.

paragraphs.text: Ryan Busse, a former gun company executive, conceded to Sam Forstag, a former firefighter and union leader, in the Democratic House primary in Montana, although The Associated Press has not called the race. Busse said in a statement that he called Forstag on Wednesday morning to congratulate him. “Now is the time to come together and fight for the Montana we all want to see,” he said. “That means helping Sam Forstag defeat radio shock jock Aaron Flint.”

Sam Forstag, a former firefighter, has declared victory in a competitive Democratic House primary in western Montana, but The Associated Press has not called the race. “We did it, Montana,” Forstag, who was joined on the campaign trail in recent days by Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, wrote on social media. He faced Ryan Busse, a former Democratic nominee for Montana governor, in a primary in a conservative-leaning district that Democrats hope to flip.

pageConfig.summary: Get live results and maps from the 2026 California primary elections.
pageConfig.headline: California Primary Election Results

text.paragraph: Primary contests will be held in six states on Tuesday. We’ll be paying close attention to California, where dozens of candidates are vying to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom in an open primary. Here’s what you need to know about the Times’s election results.

In a special election for the remainder of the term of Representative Doug LaMalfa, who died in January, James Gallagher, a Republican state assemblyman, is favored. But in the primary for the next term, held under a new map, Democrats have the advantage. State Senator Mike McGuire is favored in a race that also features Audrey Denney, an educator.

Mayor Karen Bass is facing two main challengers from opposite ends of the political spectrum: Nithya Raman, a liberal City Council member and former ally of the mayor, and Spencer Pratt, a Republican reality television star whose house burned in the Palisades fire.

Gov. Gavin Newsom cannot run again because of term limits. The leading Democrats trying to succeed him are Xavier Becerra, a former California attorney general; and Tom Steyer, a former hedge fund manager. Steve Hilton, a former Fox News host, is the top Republican.

The top two candidates in this primary will advance to the general election in November, regardless of party affiliation. Most Californians vote by mail, and ballots postmarked on or before Election Day have one week to arrive. In the 2024 primary, about half of results were reported by 5 a.m. Eastern time the day after the election. It took 10 days before 95 percent of results were reported.

If no candidate receives a majority of the vote, the top two candidates will advance to an Aug. 4 runoff. Most Californians vote by mail, and ballots postmarked on or before Election Day have one week to arrive. In the 2024 primary, about half of results were reported by 5 a.m. Eastern time the day after the election. It took 10 days before 95 percent of results were reported.

If a candidate in this race wins a majority of the primary vote, they are elected outright. Otherwise, the top two candidates advance to the general election in November. Most Californians vote by mail, and ballots postmarked on or before Election Day have one week to arrive. In the 2024 primary, about half of results were reported by 5 a.m. Eastern time the day after the election. It took 10 days before 95 percent of results were reported.

If a candidate in this race wins a majority of the primary vote, they are elected outright. Otherwise, the top two candidates advance to the general election in November. Most Californians vote by mail, and ballots postmarked on or before Election Day have one week to arrive. In the 2024 primary, about half of results were reported by 5 a.m. Eastern time the day after the election. It took 10 days before 95 percent of results were reported.

If a candidate in this race wins a majority of the primary vote, they are elected outright. Otherwise, the top two candidates advance to the general election in November. Most Californians vote by mail, and ballots postmarked on or before Election Day have one week to arrive. In the 2024 primary, about half of results were reported by 5 a.m. Eastern time the day after the election. It took 10 days before 95 percent of results were reported.
Dozens of candidates are competing in this top-two primary, including Democrats Xavier Becerra and Tom Steyer, and Steve Hilton, a Republican.
Representative Kevin Kiley, a Republican turned independent. faces a host of Democrats in this newly redrawn Sacramento-area district.
State Senator Scott Wiener, Saikat Chakrabarti and Connie Chan are competing to replace Representative Nancy Pelosi, who is retiring after decades in office.
Representative David Valadao, a Republican who is facing off against two Democrats, is newly vulnerable after redistricting.
Two Republican incumbents are facing a handful of Democrats in this district, whose boundaries were recently redrawn.
Representative Darrell Issa, a Republican targeted by redistricting, withdrew from the race, and several contenders are vying to replace him.
President Trump has endorsed Aaron Flint to replace Representative Ryan Zinke, while leading progressives have endorsed Sam Forstag.
An unexplained absence from Congress by the incumbent, Thomas Kean Jr., has overshadowed the primary. Four Democrats are vying to replace him.
Two Democratic state legislators are competing for the opportunity to face Representative Ashley Hinson, who was endorsed by President Trump.
Five Democrats are running to face Kurt Alme, a Trump-endorsed Republican, but much of the party is backing an independent in the general election.
Four Republicans are competing to run against Senator Cory Booker, who is seeking a third term and has no primary challenger.
The incumbent junior senator Ben Ray Luján, Democrat of New Mexico, is considered a heavy favorite over Matt Dodson, a democratic socialist.
The incumbent senator Mike Rounds has outraised his Republican primary challenger, Justin McNeal. The winner will face Julian Beaudion, a Democrat.
Four other Republicans are competing against Representative Randy Feenstra for the opportunity to face Rob Sand in the general election.
Three Republicans vying for their party’s nomination will likely face Deb Haaland, the favored Democratic nominee, in the general election.
Gov. Larry Rhoden, a Republican who entered office in January 2025, has three primary challengers. Dan Ahlers is unopposed as the Democrat.
Mayor Karen Bass, a Democrat, faces challenges from a progressive councilwoman, Nithya Raman, and a Republican reality television star, Spencer Pratt.
California’s congressional boundaries were redrawn last year after voters approved a map designed to favor Democrats.
Precinct-level results show how different parts of the state are voting.

pageConfig.display_credit: By The New York Times election results team: Michael Andre, Neil Berg, Matthew Bloch, Lily Boyce, Irineo Cabreros, Nico Chilla, Nate Cohn, Annie Daniel, Saurabh Datar, Andrew Fischer, Martín González Gómez, Jasmine C. Lee, Alex Lemonides, Zach Levitt, Ilana Marcus, Alicia Parlapiano, Dan Simmons-Ritchie, Caroline Soler, Charlie Smart, Luke Vrotsos and Christine Zhang. Additional contributions by Camille Baker, Kellen Browning, Sarah Cahalan, Amanda Cordero, Reid J. Epstein, Patrick Hays, Ken Iwane, Jacob Meschke, Laurel Rosenhall, Alex Shuey, Tracey Tully and Jessica White. Edited by Wilson Andrews, Lindsey Rogers Cook, William P. Davis, Kenan Davis, Amy Hughes, Ben Koski and Allison McCartney.

pollClosingTimes.poll_closing_text: Polls close at 11 p.m. Eastern time.
